---
title: "Renaming Variables"
format: html
---

Rename variables using the **dplyr** package from **tidyverse** [@tidyverse2019]. This is useful for tidying up cryptic export names (e.g., Q1_1 from Qualtrics) into something more meaningful.

``` r
library(tidyverse)
data <- data %>% 
  rename(A = q, B = e)
```

**What this is doing:**

-   `rename()` changes column names without touching any of the data inside them.

-   The syntax is always **new name = old name** — so `A = q` renames the column currently called `q` to `A`, and `B = e` renames `e` to `B`.

You can rename as many columns as you like in one call by adding more `new_name = old_name` pairs, separated by commas:

``` r
data <- data %>%
  rename(A = q,
         B = e,
         C = f,
         D = z)
```

## References
